verdict: Beyond the rate-map shearing of #5d, the NEURAL MANIFOLD of the standard continuous attractor does NOT deform: trapezoid population codes lie on the same manifold as the square's (overlap ~ the square-vs-square reference), a rigid torus consistent with Gardner 2022 -- so #5d is a warping of the space->manifold MAP, not the manifold. In a non-Euclidean (barrier) environment the fixed grid ignores the wall (neural distance ~ Euclidean) while a PLASTIC code reshapes to the geodesic geometry (respects the wall). Manifold deformation to the environment's actual geometry requires plasticity, which the rigid CAN lacks.
